Entity_Description: Kent is a district municipality of about 6,300 people (2021 Census) on the north bank of the Fraser River in British Columbia's Fraser Valley, roughly 99 km east of Vancouver. Its town centre is Agassiz, a farming community known for corn and dairy production and home to a long-established Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada research farm. Kent's flat floodplain fields spread out beneath Mount Cheam, and the Agassiz-Rosedale Bridge on Highway 9 links the district across the Fraser toward Chilliwack. The federal Kent and Mountain Institutions are also located within the municipality.

Crews reach most Kent addresses within {RESPONSE_TIME}, and also serve Harrison Hot Springs and Chilliwack via the Agassiz-Rosedale Bridge. Call {PHONE}.
Short_Description: Kent is the Fraser Valley's quiet cornfield capital: the district wraps around Agassiz, where corn stands, dairy barns, and a federal agricultural research farm have anchored the local economy for generations. That working-farm character shapes what property owners here actually need from {SERVICE} — acreages with long driveways, outbuildings, and homes that take the full force of the valley's wet winters. {COMPANY_NAME} is a {BUSINESS_TYPE} that treats Kent as its own market, not an afterthought tacked onto Chilliwack: we know the difference between a heritage farmhouse off Highway 9 and a newer home in the Agassiz townsite, and we schedule around the realities of rural properties on well and septic. Whether you're under Mount Cheam's shadow or out toward Harrison Hot Springs, get {SERVICE} done right — call {PHONE}.
Long_Description:
Ask anyone in the Fraser Valley where the corn comes from and they will point across the Agassiz-Rosedale Bridge to Kent. This district municipality of about 6,300 people occupies the flat, fertile north bank of the Fraser River, with the town of Agassiz at its centre and the massive wall of Mount Cheam rising directly across the water. It is a place where a federal agricultural research farm, dairy operations, and roadside corn stands still define daily life — even as commuters and retirees discover that Kent offers small-town space within reach of Chilliwack, just 16 km away.

That mix shapes the property landscape a {BUSINESS_TYPE} works with here. Kent's building stock runs from heritage farmhouses and working acreages — many on well and septic rather than municipal services — to the compact residential blocks of the Agassiz townsite and newer infill homes. Outbuildings, barns, long gravel driveways, and large lots are the norm outside the town centre, and they demand a different approach to {SERVICE} than a standard suburban property. {COMPANY_NAME} plans every Kent job around what is actually on the ground, not a template.

Climate matters just as much. Kent sits in the eastern Fraser Valley, where winters bring heavier rain than Metro Vancouver along with occasional outflow freezes and snow funnelling down the valley — and the November 2021 atmospheric river that flooded parts of the region made drainage and water management a household topic from Agassiz to Sumas Prairie. Floodplain properties here reward maintenance done before the fall rains arrive, and {COMPANY_NAME} builds that seasonal rhythm into how we deliver {SERVICE}.

Geography also defines coverage. Kent is the hinge of the valley's north bank: Agassiz sits at its heart, Harrison Hot Springs is only 8 km up the road, and the Agassiz-Rosedale Bridge — the lone Fraser crossing between Mission and Hope — connects the district to Chilliwack, Sardis, and Vedder Crossing. When you book {SERVICE} with {COMPANY_NAME}, you get a team that already runs these routes, understands {PROPERTY_TYPE} needs on both banks of the river, and answers with a {ESTIMATE_TYPE} instead of guesswork. Call {PHONE} to get started.
Local_Facts:
- Population: 6,300 (2021 Census), within the Fraser Valley Regional District
- District municipality on the north bank of the Fraser River; its town centre is Agassiz
- Mount Cheam rises directly across the Fraser from Kent's floodplain farmland
- Agriculture anchors the economy: corn and dairy farming, plus a federal Agriculture and Agri-Food Canada research farm in Agassiz
- The Agassiz-Rosedale Bridge (Highway 9) is the only road crossing of the Fraser between Mission and Hope
- Home to two federal correctional facilities, Kent Institution and Mountain Institution
- Roughly 99 km east of Vancouver; nearest neighbours are Agassiz (town centre), Harrison Hot Springs (8 km), and Chilliwack (16 km)
